contentBlocks.original:
जगज्जालपालं कनत्कण्ठमालं
शरच्चन्द्रफालं महादैत्यकालम् ।
नभोनीलकायं दुरावारमायं
सुपद्मासहायं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagajjālapālaṃ kanatkaṇṭhamālaṃ
śarachchandraphālaṃ mahādaityakālam ।
nabhōnīlakāyaṃ durāvāramāyaṃ
supadmāsahāyaṃ bhajē'haṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sustainer of the web of the world, wearing a radiant throat-garland, whose forehead gleams like the autumn moon, the Kala (destroyer) of great demons — of sky-blue hue, of unfathomable maya, the consort of lotus-seated Lakshmi — I salute Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
सदाम्भोधिवासं गलत्पुष्पहासं
जगत्सन्निवासं शतादित्यभासम् ।
गदाचक्रशस्त्रं लसत्पीतवस्त्रं
हसच्चारुवक्त्रं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadāmbhōdhivāsaṃ galatpuṣpahāsaṃ
jagatsannivāsaṃ śatādityabhāsam ।
gadāchakraśastraṃ lasatpītavastraṃ
hasachchāruvaktraṃ bhajē'haṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ever dwelling in the ocean, with a garland of lotus blossoms gracing him, the abode of the universe, blazing like a hundred suns — bearing mace and discus, wearing shining yellow garments, with a smiling countenance — I salute Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
रमाकण्ठहारं श्रुतिव्रातसारं
जलान्तर्विहारं धराभारहारम् ।
चिदानन्दरूपं मनोज्ञस्वरूपं
धृतानेकरूपं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ramākaṇṭhahāraṃ śrutivrātasāraṃ
jalāntarvihāraṃ dharābhārahāram ।
chidānandarūpaṃ manōjñasvarūpaṃ
dhṛtānēkarūpaṃ bhajē'haṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who wears Lakshmi's garland at his throat, the very essence of all the Vedas, who sports within the cosmic waters, who removes the burden of the earth — of the form of pure consciousness-bliss, of beautiful form, who assumed many avatars — I salute Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
जराजन्महीनं परानन्दपीनं
समाधानलीनं सदैवानवीनम् ।
जगज्जन्महेतुं सुरानीककेतुं
त्रिलोकैकसेतुं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jarājanmahīnaṃ parānandapīnaṃ
samādhānalīnaṃ sadaivānavīnam ।
jagajjanmahētuṃ surānīkakētuṃ
trilōkaikasētuṃ bhajē'haṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Free from old age and birth, full of supreme bliss — ever absorbed in samadhi, ever fresh and new — the cause of the world's creation, the banner-Lord of the gods, the sole bridge of the three worlds — I salute Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
कृताम्नायगानं खगाधीशयानं
विमुक्तेर्निदानं हरारातिमानम् ।
स्वभक्तानुकूलं जगद्वृक्षमूलं
निरस्तार्तशूलं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛtāmnāyagānaṃ khagādhīśayānaṃ
vimuktērnidānaṃ harārātimānam ।
svabhaktānukūlaṃ jagadvṛkṣamūlaṃ
nirastārtaśūlaṃ bhajē'haṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who sang forth the Vedas, whose mount is Garuda king of birds, the very source of liberation — ever favorable to his own devotees, the root of the world-tree, remover of the arrow of affliction — I salute Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तामरेशं द्विरेफाभकेशं
जगद्बिम्बलेशं हृदाकाशवेशम् ।
सदा दिव्यदेहं विमुक्ताखिलेहं
सुवैकुण्ठगेहं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastāmarēśaṃ dvirēphābhakēśaṃ
jagadbimbalēśaṃ hṛdākāśavēśam ।
sadā divyadēhaṃ vimuktākhilēhaṃ
suvaikuṇṭhagēhaṃ bhajē'haṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Lord of all the gods, whose dark hair is circled by bees, a fragment of the world's reflection, whose dwelling is the sky of the heart — always of a divine body, freed from all cravings, whose home is the beautiful Vaikuntha — I salute Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
सुरालीबलिष्ठं त्रिलोकीवरिष्ठं
गुरूणां गरिष्ठं स्वरूपैकनिष्ठम् ।
सदा युद्धधीरं महावीरवीरं
भवाम्भोधितीरं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
surālībaliṣṭhaṃ trilōkīvariṣṭhaṃ
gurūṇāṃ gariṣṭhaṃ svarūpaikaniṣṭham ।
sadā yuddhadhīraṃ mahāvīravīraṃ
bhavāmbhōdhitīraṃ bhajē'haṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The mightiest among the hosts of gods, the greatest in the three worlds, the most weighty of gurus — ever established in his own true nature — ever courageous in battle, most heroic of heroes, the ship to cross the ocean of becoming — I salute Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
रमावामभागं तलालग्ननागं
कृताधीनयागं गतारागरागम् ।
मुनीन्द्रैस्सुगीतं सुरैस्सम्परीतं
गुणौघैरतीतं भजेऽहं भजेऽहम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ramāvāmabhāgaṃ talālagnanāgaṃ
kṛtādhīnayāgaṃ gatārāgarāgam ।
munīndraissugītaṃ suraissamparītaṃ
guṇaughairatītaṃ bhajē'haṃ bhajē'ham ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whose left side is Lakshmi (Rama), on whose feet the serpent Adishesha rests, for whom yagas (sacrifices) are lovingly performed — free from all attachment, praised in song by great sages, surrounded by the gods, beyond all qualities — I salute Hari.

contentBlocks.original:
फलश्रुति ।
इदं यस्तु नित्यं समाधाय चित्तं
पठेदष्टकं कण्ठहारं मुरारेः ।
स विष्णोर्विशोकं ध्रुवं याति लोकं
जराजन्मशोकं पुनर्विन्दते नो ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalaśruti ।
idaṃ yastu nityaṃ samādhāya chittaṃ
paṭhēdaṣṭakaṃ kaṇṭhahāraṃ murārēḥ ।
sa viṣṇōrviśōkaṃ dhruvaṃ yāti lōkaṃ
jarājanmaśōkaṃ punarvindatē nō ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One who daily with a concentrated mind recites this ashtakam — the garland of Murari (Krishna) — will without doubt attain the sorrowless state of Vishnu. (Phalashruti)
Thus ends the Sri Hari Stotram composed by Sri Paramahamsa Swami Brahmananda. (Colophon)
